prose:
**Formaldehyde Fukui Reactivity Analysis** — DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP), PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: fixed input from user-supplied .xyz file; single-point property calculation (not relaxed at DFT). SCF converged, all Fukui index charge-conservation checks passed (integrity status: ok, trustworthy=true). Mulliken partial charges and Fukui condensation indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, dual descriptor) computed for all 4 atoms (O, C, H, H). **Most electrophilic site:** Carbon (atom 2, f⁺ = 0.7084), predicted susceptible to nucleophilic attack. **Most nucleophilic site:** Oxygen (atom 1, f⁻ = 0.4674), predicted susceptible to electrophilic attack. Dual descriptor at C = 0.5959 (strong electrophilic preference); at O = 0.0007 (balanced). Hydrogen atoms show low reactivity (negative dual descriptors). Output: result JSON at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpj78x4xlg/live.json`; PNG bar chart at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/argo_claude-haiku-4.5/20260704-181847_formaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`.</prose>
<parameter name="integrity_trustworthy">true
